key_highlights: Gold extends gains as Warsh comments ease rate-hike prospects, signaling dovish Fed stance.
Tech stocks slump globally, led by AI jitters and chip selloff, with Korean stocks tumbling 8%.
Yen strengthens sharply amid intervention risks, while oil slides on increased Hormuz flows.
Trump won't renew USMCA, toppling trade stability, and announces $1.4B in crypto earnings.
US jobs report preview shows stable hiring but warning signs, with Treasuries slipping ahead.
Anthropic releases Claude Science and Fable 5 AI model, while Microsoft cuts under 2.5% workforce.
Switzerland inflation slows for first time in eight months, easing global inflation concerns.
BlackRock and KKR join Mexico infrastructure talks, signaling emerging market opportunities.

sectors.trend: Broad selloff driven by AI capacity glut fears and Meta's comments, with chip stocks plunging across Asia (Korean stocks -8%) and US tech retreating ahead of payrolls. Leveraged ETFs amplify AI bets, adding volatility.
sectors.implications: Short-term correction in overvalued AI names; long-term AI infrastructure demand remains strong but near-term oversupply risks. Investors should reduce exposure to high-beta AI stocks and consider value tech.

sectors.trend: Oil extends slide as more barrels flow through Hormuz, easing supply fears. Gold gains on dovish Fed comments. Iran's floating oil hoard swells as buyers stay away.
sectors.implications: Lower oil prices benefit consumers and transport sectors but hurt energy stocks. Gold remains attractive as a hedge against rate uncertainty. Monitor Hormuz developments for supply shocks.

sectors.trend: Treasuries slip before payrolls as traders weigh Warsh comments. Yen strengthens sharply on intervention risks. Dollar optimism challenged by Morgan Stanley and others.
sectors.implications: Bond yields may rise if payrolls surprise strong; yen intervention risk high. Dollar weakness could boost emerging markets. Investors should duration-hedge and consider yen longs.

sectors.trend: Nike earnings expected after bell, with consumer spending under pressure from 'funflation' and heat waves. Amazon launches $1B FDE org, while American Airlines adds grab-and-go lounges.
sectors.implications: Consumer discretionary stocks face headwinds from inflation and weather disruptions. Nike's results will be a key barometer. Focus on resilient brands and travel-related plays.

sectors.trend: Anthropic releases Claude Science for pharma research; FDA allows ZYN pouches on health benefits. Private equity ownership of vet clinics draws scrutiny.
sectors.implications: AI in drug discovery is a growing theme; ZYN approval boosts tobacco alternatives. Regulatory scrutiny on PE in healthcare may limit M&A. Favor innovative biotech and AI-health plays.

sectors.trend: China stocks lifted by tech giants, but chip selloff hits Asia. India ramps up share sales to cushion oil budget hit; Brazil opens new rail route for soymeal exports. Mexico infrastructure talks with BlackRock/KKR.
sectors.implications: China tech selective opportunity; India and Brazil benefit from infrastructure and export diversification. Mexico infrastructure play is long-term positive. Monitor US-China tariff talks on agriculture.

key_themes: AI bubble fears and rotation out of tech
Dovish Fed pivot and gold rally
Oil supply stability and geopolitical risks
Consumer strain from 'funflation' and heat waves
Regulatory shifts in AI, healthcare, and banking

risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce overweight in high-beta AI stocks; diversify into value and defensive sectors; use stop-losses on leveraged ETF positions.
Hedge with gold and defensive currencies; reduce exposure to trade-sensitive sectors (autos, agriculture); monitor USMCA renegotiation.
Avoid short yen positions; consider yen-denominated assets or hedges; monitor BOJ actions closely.
Maintain some energy exposure as hedge; monitor Iran and Strait of Hormuz developments; oil prices may spike if tensions escalate.
Prepare for bond yield volatility; position in short-duration Treasuries if data weak; consider floating-rate notes.
Avoid overexposure to energy-intensive sectors; favor utilities with grid investments; monitor agricultural commodity prices.
Hayfin's €15B fund shows resilience; but monitor US bill that could drain $1.3T from local banks. Diversify credit exposure.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy gold and gold miners on dips
opportunities.rationale: Dovish Fed comments and rate-hike easing support gold. Geopolitical risks and trade uncertainty add safe-haven demand.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate select AI infrastructure plays after selloff
opportunities.rationale: Long-term AI demand for electricity and data centers remains strong; near-term oversupply fears create entry points for quality names.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in emerging market infrastructure (India, Brazil, Mexico)
opportunities.rationale: Infrastructure deals (Adani, BlackRock/KKR) and export route expansions signal growth. India's share sale plan and Brazil's rail route boost long-term prospects.

opportunities.recommendation: Go long yen via ETFs or futures
opportunities.rationale: Yen intervention risk and sharp gain signal potential for further appreciation. Natixis says move is unlikely a market movement, suggesting official support.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Swiss franc as inflation hedge
opportunities.rationale: Switzerland's inflation slowing for first time in eight months supports franc strength and safe-haven status.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce tech exposure and rotate to defensive sectors (utilities, healthcare)
defensive_moves.rationale: AI selloff and chip slump indicate near-term downside. Utilities benefit from grid emergency and AI electricity demand. Healthcare offers stability.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against US trade policy risk with gold and short USMCA-exposed sectors
defensive_moves.rationale: Trump's refusal to renew USMCA and tariff refund delays create uncertainty. Avoid auto and agriculture stocks.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase cash or short-duration bonds ahead of payrolls
defensive_moves.rationale: Payrolls data could trigger bond volatility. Short-duration Treasuries reduce duration risk while providing yield.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid leveraged ETFs and high-beta AI names
defensive_moves.rationale: Leveraged ETFs amplify losses in a selloff. AI jitters and Meta's capacity glut comments suggest further downside.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Monitor and reduce exposure to consumer discretionary amid 'funflation'
defensive_moves.rationale: Rising live event prices and heat waves strain consumer budgets. Nike earnings will be a key indicator; if weak, reduce further.

market_outlook.short_term: Markets are in a corrective phase driven by AI profit-taking, trade policy uncertainty, and currency volatility. The US payrolls report will be a key catalyst; a strong print could lift yields and pressure equities, while a weak print may reinforce dovish Fed bets. Gold and yen are likely to remain supported. Oil may stabilize but faces downside risk from increased Hormuz flows. Tech selloff could deepen before a bottom forms.
market_outlook.long_term: AI infrastructure demand remains a multi-year growth driver, but near-term oversupply and valuation concerns warrant caution. Emerging markets, especially India and Brazil, offer structural growth opportunities. US trade policy under Trump (USMCA non-renewal, tariffs) will create persistent uncertainty. Inflation is moderating globally (Switzerland, US), supporting a gradual Fed pivot. Private credit and infrastructure investments are attractive for yield and diversification.
key_catalysts: Fed minutes and Warsh comments on rate path
Nike earnings and consumer spending data
AI earnings from major tech companies (Meta, Microsoft)
Oil supply disruptions from Hormuz or Iran
European heat wave impact on agriculture and energy
India budget and share sale plans
watch_list: US 10-year Treasury yield and breakeven inflation rates
S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 technical support levels
Oil inventories and Strait of Hormuz transit data
AI-related ETF flows and short interest
Swiss franc and yen as safe-haven proxies
Regulatory decisions on AI models (Anthropic Fable 5) and stablecoins
